options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: goal
options.description:
Choose the main goal.
complete_all_levels: All levels of the selected episodes
complete_boss_levels: Boss levels (E#M8) of selected episodes

options.name: difficulty
options.description:
Choose the game difficulty. These options match DOOM's skill levels.
baby (I'm too young to die.) Same as easy, with double ammo pickups and half damage taken.
easy (Hey, not too rough.) Less monsters or strength.
medium (Hurt me plenty.) Default.
hard (Ultra-Violence.) More monsters or strength.
nightmare (Nightmare!) Monsters attack more rapidly and respawn.

options.name: random_monsters
options.description:
Choose how monsters are randomized.
vanilla: No randomization
shuffle: Monsters are shuffled within the level
random_balanced: Monsters are completely randomized, but balanced based on existing ratio in the level. (Small monsters vs medium vs big)
random_chaotic: Monsters are completely randomized, but balanced based on existing ratio in the entire game.

options.name: random_pickups
options.description:
Choose how pickups are randomized.
vanilla: No randomization
shuffle: Pickups are shuffled within the level
random_balanced: Pickups are completely randomized, but balanced based on existing ratio in the level. (Small pickups vs Big)

options.name: random_music
options.description:
Level musics will be randomized.
vanilla: No randomization
shuffle_selected: Selected episodes' levels will be shuffled
shuffle_game: All the music will be shuffled

options.name: flip_levels
options.description:
Flip levels on one axis.
vanilla: No flipping
flipped: All levels are flipped
randomly_flipped: Random levels are flipped

options.name: allow_death_logic
options.description:
Some locations require a timed puzzle that can only be tried once.
After which, if the player failed to get it, the location cannot be checked anymore.
By default, no progression items are placed here. There is a way, hovewer, to still get them:
Get killed in the current map. The map will reset, you can now attempt the puzzle again.

options.name: pro
options.description:
Include difficult tricks into rules. Mostly employed by speed runners.
i.e.: Leaps across to a locked area, trigger a switch behind a window at the right angle, etc.

options.name: start_with_computer_area_maps
options.description: Give the player all Computer Area Map items from the start.

options.name: death_link
options.description: When you die, everyone who enabled death link dies. Of course, the reverse is true too.

options.name: reset_level_on_death
options.description:
When dying, levels are reset and monsters respawned. But inventory and checks are kept.
Turning this setting off is considered easy mode. Good for new players that don't know the levels well.

options.name: episode1
options.description:
Knee-Deep in the Dead.
If none of the episodes are chosen, Episode 1 will be chosen by default.

options.name: episode2
options.description:
The Shores of Hell.
If none of the episodes are chosen, Episode 1 will be chosen by default.

options.name: episode3
options.description:
Inferno.
If none of the episodes are chosen, Episode 1 will be chosen by default.

options.name: episode4
options.description:
Thy Flesh Consumed.
If none of the episodes are chosen, Episode 1 will be chosen by default.

options.name: split_backpack
options.description: Split the Backpack into four individual items, each one increasing ammo capacity for one type of weapon only.

options.name: backpack_count
options.description:
How many Backpacks will be available.
If Split Backpack is set, this will be the number of each capacity upgrade available.

options.name: max_ammo_bullets
options.description: Set the starting ammo capacity for bullets.

options.name: max_ammo_shells
options.description: Set the starting ammo capacity for shotgun shells.

options.name: max_ammo_rockets
options.description: Set the starting ammo capacity for rockets.

options.name: max_ammo_energy_cells
options.description: Set the starting ammo capacity for energy cells.

options.name: added_ammo_bullets
options.description: Set the amount of bullet capacity added when collecting a backpack or capacity upgrade.

options.name: added_ammo_shells
options.description: Set the amount of shotgun shell capacity added when collecting a backpack or capacity upgrade.

options.name: added_ammo_rockets
options.description: Set the amount of rocket capacity added when collecting a backpack or capacity upgrade.

options.name: added_ammo_energy_cells
options.description: Set the amount of energy cell capacity added when collecting a backpack or capacity upgrade.

options.description:
Start with the specified amount of these items and don't place them in the world. Example: "Bomb: 1"

The game decides what the replacement items will be.
